title: Ardisia depauperata
taxonomy: Ardisia depauperata is classified within the family Myrsinaceae, which is a family of flowering plants in the order Ericales. The species Ardisia depauperata is native to tropical Asia and is part of the Ardisia genus, which includes over 700 species of plants. The classification of Ardisia depauperata is further divided into kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, and order Ericales. This classification indicates the plant's evolutionary relationships and position within the plant kingdom. Ardisia depauperata's classification is a key aspect of understanding its biology and ecology.

intro: Ardisia depauperata, also known as the small bittersweet or bittersweet shrub, is a type of plant in the family Primulaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Ardisia depauperata include iridoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties.
